executionModelDetail: Direct in-session feature build (parent-expanded). SCOPE-01 implemented + unit-proven this session; SCOPE-02 is the live delivery proof gated on a new CI image build + home-lab redeploy.
inProgressReason: Both scopes are complete with real evidence. SCOPE-01 (Google Calendar write client + SST config + secret-key 3-mirror + scheduler wiring) is unit-proven: 15 gcal-client tests PASS, config fail-loud PASS, secret-key contract PASS (home-lab bundle emits the placeholder for CARD_REWARDS_GCAL_CREDENTIALS, no literal leak), format + lint clean. SCOPE-02 (live home-lab delivery) is proven on the real host: commit fc931c6a CI-built + cosign-signed the new images + home-lab bundle; the gcal credential was sops/age-encrypted into the knb adapter (knb 1856ca3); apply.sh --trust-model=ci-keyless redeployed core 73dd65dd with substituted_secret_count=7 + calendar_sync=true + 'production Google Calendar delivery wired' in the boot log + core/ml verify healthy; and the deployed GoogleCalendarClient code wrote a real event to the operator's 'Credit cards' calendar (insert -> idempotent no-dup re-sync -> delete) against the live Google Calendar API. Status is held at in_progress (NOT promoted to full-delivery done) pending the heavy full-delivery certification ceremony (regression/chaos/audit/security/harden/gaps/simplify/docs specialist phases + their report sections) which is not all genuinely applicable to this focused 1-feature build; the engineering + live proof are done.

scopeProgress.name: Google Calendar write client + config + wiring

executionHistory.note: Grounded the gap: the card-rewards CardCalendarBridge (spec 083) is fully built but its CalDAVClient is never constructed (wiring passes nil + warns 'production CalDAV client is not wired yet'). The only Google Calendar usage in the tree is the READ path (internal/connector/caldav GETs /events) — no event-WRITE client exists. Confirmed smackerel's tokenStore + OAuth handler exist but request calendar.readonly scope; the operator's recovered CCManager credential has full calendar write scope, live-verified (refresh OK, owner on the target calendar). Chose to build a focused write client in the cardrewards package + deliver the credential as the managed secret CARD_REWARDS_GCAL_CREDENTIALS.

SCOPE-02 live delivery proven. Committed fc931c6a (Google Calendar write client + wiring + 3-mirror secret + config); CI build.yml built + cosign-signed core 73dd65dd + ml 909719f4 + home-lab bundle (build-clients Android lane failed on operator-private keystore, unrelated). sops/age-encrypted the gcal credential JSON into knb smackerel/secrets/home-lab.enc.env (ciphertext only, value never in tree); knb params calendar_sync=true + calendar_id + apply.sh CARD_REWARDS_CALENDAR_ID emit (knb 1856ca3). Redeployed via apply.sh --trust-model=ci-keyless on the host (sudo, matching the operator=root precedent so the sudo-aware age resolver finds the key + root reads the root-owned manifest): cosign verified, bundle sha matched, substituted_secret_count=7, CARD_REWARDS calendar_sync=true emitted, core+ml recreated + verify healthy, digests match, apply OK. Boot log: 'card-rewards scheduler: production Google Calendar delivery wired'. Proved the DEPLOYED GoogleCalendarClient code end-to-end against the real 'Credit cards' calendar with the operator's real credential: insert -> idempotent same-uid re-sync (1 event, no dup) -> delete (0). Throwaway proof harness cmd/cardrewards-calproof deleted (never committed). No secret value logged anywhere.

scopeProgress.name: Google Calendar write client + config + wiring
